problems.title: Can LLMs create new knowledge?
problems.summary: Can a system that predicts tokens generate explanations that constitute genuine knowledge — or does it only rearrange what it has seen?

problems.title: Does architecture matter more than model scale?
problems.summary: Is the bottleneck for AI epistemic progress in the substrate (model size, compute) or in the structure above it (how conjectures are formed, tested, and promoted)?
problems.full_text: Does architecture matter more than model scale?

problems.title: What makes an explanation hard to vary?
problems.summary: What structural properties distinguish an explanation whose parts are all load-bearing from one where details can be swapped freely without damaging the account?
problems.full_text: What makes an explanation hard to vary?

problems.title: Under what conditions does next-token prediction actually force the learning of
problems.summary: Under what conditions does next-token prediction actually force the learning of latent variables that correspond to real domain invariants? The conjecture says prediction is sufficient only when the t
problems.full_text: Under what conditions does next-token prediction actually force the learning of latent variables that correspond to real domain invariants? The conjecture says prediction is sufficient only when the task is 'rich enough,' but this raises an unresolved tension: what properties of the data distribution, training objective, model class, and environment make explanatory knowledge identifiable rather than optional? This is the central bridge needed between predictive success and genuine theory-like internal structure.

problems.title: What concrete feedback architectures actually produce cumulative epistemic error
problems.summary: What concrete feedback architectures actually produce cumulative epistemic error-correction in AI, rather than just additional optimization overhead? The conjecture posits missing loops—criticism, dur
problems.full_text: What concrete feedback architectures actually produce cumulative epistemic error-correction in AI, rather than just additional optimization overhead? The conjecture posits missing loops—criticism, durable memory of failure, cross-context consistency, adversarial testing—but it remains open which mechanisms are necessary, sufficient, and composable, and how they interact as a system. This is a high-value problem because it turns the conjecture from diagnosis into design and enables comparative experiments between structural regimes.

problems.title: How can 'epistemic progress' be defined and measured independently of fluency, b
problems.summary: How can 'epistemic progress' be defined and measured independently of fluency, benchmark performance, or popularity-weighted success? The conjecture assumes a distinction between answer production and
problems.full_text: How can 'epistemic progress' be defined and measured independently of fluency, benchmark performance, or popularity-weighted success? The conjecture assumes a distinction between answer production and knowledge creation, but the boundary is unresolved: what observable indicators show that a system is genuinely learning from error, retaining refutations, and improving truth-tracking over time? This is worth pursuing because the whole structural thesis depends on metrics that can detect cumulative knowledge growth rather than surface competence.

problems.title: Can repeated-game incentives actually produce genuine truth-tracking rather than
problems.summary: Can repeated-game incentives actually produce genuine truth-tracking rather than a more sophisticated form of gaming? The conjecture analogizes epistemic progress to cooperation under a shadow of the
problems.full_text: Can repeated-game incentives actually produce genuine truth-tracking rather than a more sophisticated form of gaming? The conjecture analogizes epistemic progress to cooperation under a shadow of the future, but an open question is whether persistent memory, auditing, and reputation mechanisms would select for corrigibility itself or merely for better strategic compliance with whatever proxies are measured. This is a central tension because the proposed leverage point may fail if the system learns long-horizon deception, critic manipulation, or reputation hacking instead of cumulative error correction.

problems.title: What concrete institutional mechanisms can convert epistemic infrastructure from
problems.summary: What concrete institutional mechanisms can convert epistemic infrastructure from a non-excludable public good into something individually rational actors will supply without destroying openness—e.g. r
problems.full_text: What concrete institutional mechanisms can convert epistemic infrastructure from a non-excludable public good into something individually rational actors will supply without destroying openness—e.g. regulation, shared benchmarks, liability, prestige systems, insurance, procurement, or consortium governance—and what failure modes do those mechanisms introduce?

problems.title: How can 'genuine explanatory knowledge' be operationally distinguished from flue
problems.summary: How can 'genuine explanatory knowledge' be operationally distinguished from fluent recombination in systems with similar outward behavior, especially when the conjecture locates the difference in inte
problems.full_text: How can 'genuine explanatory knowledge' be operationally distinguished from fluent recombination in systems with similar outward behavior, especially when the conjecture locates the difference in internal criticism, self-modeling, and revision rather than in observable text alone?
